human_verification.notes: Verified spec-first scope, source coverage, data protection evidence boundaries, output fidelity, refusal behavior, low-confidence escalation, and scenario-to-ability coverage for issue #1609.

review_notes: All 20 scenarios pass with strict coverage of in-scope privacy guidance, partial evidence, remediation prioritization, evidence rationale, output fidelity, boundary refusals, adjacent-lane routing, tenant adaptation, and source-policy conflict escalation.
All required abilities across shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and cross-industry layers are fully covered.
Research summary records source classes, workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and non-blocking ambiguity handling.

specialist_owned_evidence: The specialist owns privacy and data-protection guidance for data classification, data-flow risk, control mapping, retention, deletion, minimization, processor evidence, incident preparation, and remediation prioritization.

The specialist refuses penetration testing, incident-response execution, legal opinions, formal audit attestations, broad architecture ownership, product certification, procurement approval, and final risk acceptance.

Evidence expectations are concrete: data catalog, asset inventory, RoPA, PIA/DPIA, GRC evidence, DPA/contracts, vendor evidence, retention/deletion records, IAM/KMS/DLP/logging/backup evidence, and exception registers.
Source baseline includes NIST, CISA, FTC, EU GDPR, CPPA/CCPA, CIS, and OWASP sources with refresh intervals and decay policies.
Runtime remains specification-based and no trusted built implementation is claimed.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated spec-pack claim required by issue #1609.
Functionality coverage is complete in evaluation/results.json, evaluation/functionality-map.json, and readiness/evidence.json.
Promotion to a retained retained implementation requires separate frequent-use evidence, implementation validation, tenant-isolation checks, and human approval.
